_comment: Tracks which Fidelity export files (from ~/Downloads) have most recently been ingested into spending_summary.xlsx. Fidelity's export filenames are not reliable type indicators — 'historic' and 'pending' exports can share the same base name (e.g. TransactionHistory.csv vs TransactionHistory (4).csv). Type must be determined from file content: pending exports have Completion date == 'Pending' on every row, Status values of Priced/Ordered, and an extra 'Expiry Date' column; historic exports have real completion dates and Status values of Completed/Cancelled with no 'Expiry Date' column. On each check, only files newer (by mtime) than the entries below count as new.

pending.note: Captured for tracking only — current spending_summary.py logic filters to Status == 'Completed', so pending-status rows are not yet consumed by any calculation. Revisit if pending transactions need to feed into holdings/spend figures.
